mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.75% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 302, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.83% and CPIAUCSL at 302.918 indicate restrictive stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS+CPIAUCSL interaction and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100,000 persons month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where PAYEMS declines exceed 100k, or Rank IC between PAYEMS and forward returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand and corporate revenue growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as momentum traders react to demand signals.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 102.5 while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 3.6%, the economy enters a supply-constrained slowdown phase that reduces profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.0258 and UNRATE at 3.4%
regime_hint: stagflation risk or supply shock aftermath
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE shows Rank IC below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1,400 thousand units signal weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,420 thousand units, near threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening with rising mortgage rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 62% of months where HOUST < 1400, or Rank IC between HOUST and forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate of 25 basis points or more signals the beginning of a monetary easing cycle that reduces equity discount rates and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by 25 basis points or more month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines 25bp+, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS momentum and forward returns is below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 3.8% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth slows to below 100k monthly additions, the labor market enters a cooling phase that signals reduced consumer purchasing power and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.4% and PAYEMS at 155,673 thousand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 56% of triggered instances, or the interaction Rank IC between UNRATE and PAYEMS momentum falls below 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 20,235.878 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 58% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
